content.query:
----------------------------------------------------------
--In this usecase, We are learning about OPENROWSET, JSON data parsing using JSON_VALUE & CROSS APPLY OPENJSON, DELTA & PARQUERY format also
----------------------------------------------------------
--SQL Script to work in Azure Synapse Serverless Warehouse
----------------------------------------------------------

--database (logical container of related schemas/external tables/) creation
--Default schema where our tables are referred (DBO - Data Base owner)
create database wd36db;

--https (Object/Blob Storage & Slow)
--Openrowset (Instructing SQL engine to not consider the data as a traditional inline data in the SQL DB, rather it is in a open filesystem of set of files and produce result in a form of rows) will instruct Synapse to access the large volume of data as a rowset using the serverless SQL engine 
select * from OPENROWSET(
    BULK 'https://we47toolstorage.dfs.core.windows.net/adfcontainer/sourcedir/productdata.txt',--Blob uri
    FORMAT = 'CSV',
    PARSER_VERSION = '2.0'
) as data1;

--abfss (ADLS Gen2 & Fast)
select * from OPENROWSET(
    BULK 'abfss://adfcontainer@we47toolstorage.dfs.core.windows.net/sourcedir/productdata.txt', --ADLS uri
    FORMAT = 'CSV',
    PARSER_VERSION = '2.0'
) as data1;


--header row as column names(HEADER_ROW)

select * from OPENROWSET( --like a Dataframe
    BULK 'abfss://adfcontainer@we47toolstorage.dfs.core.windows.net/sourcedir/productdata.txt',--location
    FORMAT = 'CSV',--spark.read.csv
    HEADER_ROW = TRUE,--header=True in spark
    PARSER_VERSION = '2.0'--spark version
) as data1;

--Accessing only specific columns
select amt from OPENROWSET(
    BULK 'abfss://adfcontainer@we47toolstorage.dfs.core.windows.net/sourcedir/productdata.txt',
    FORMAT = 'CSV',
    HEADER_ROW = TRUE,
    PARSER_VERSION = '2.0'
) as data1;

--filter data
select  * from OPENROWSET(
    BULK 'abfss://adfcontainer@we47toolstorage.dfs.core.windows.net/sourcedir/productdata.txt',
    FORMAT = 'CSV',
    HEADER_ROW = TRUE,
    PARSER_VERSION = '2.0'
) as data1
where amt>300;

--group by (de duplicating)
select * from OPENROWSET(
    BULK 'abfss://adfcontainer@we47toolstorage.dfs.core.windows.net/sourcedir/productdata.txt',
    FORMAT = 'CSV',
    HEADER_ROW = TRUE,
    PARSER_VERSION = '2.0'
) as data1
group by prodid,amt;

--aggregation
select  sum(amt) from OPENROWSET(
    BULK 'abfss://adfcontainer@we47toolstorage.dfs.core.windows.net/sourcedir/productdata.txt',
    FORMAT = 'CSV',
    HEADER_ROW = TRUE,
    PARSER_VERSION = '2.0'
) as data1;

select  * from OPENROWSET(
    BULK 'abfss://adfcontainer@we47toolstorage.dfs.core.windows.net/sourcedir/productdata.txt',
    FORMAT = 'CSV',
    HEADER_ROW = TRUE,
    PARSER_VERSION = '2.0'
) as data1
inner join 
OPENROWSET(
    BULK 'abfss://adfcontainer@we47toolstorage.dfs.core.windows.net/sourcedir/productdata.txt',
    FORMAT = 'CSV',
    HEADER_ROW = TRUE,
    PARSER_VERSION = '2.0'
) as data2
on data1.prodid=data2.prodid;

--setting the column names using WITH clause (Schema Evolution or Enrichment or with column function)
select prodid,'2026-05-16' loaddt,amt from OPENROWSET(
    BULK 'https://we47toolstorage.dfs.core.windows.net/adfcontainer/sourcedir/productdata.txt',
    FORMAT = 'CSV',
    PARSER_VERSION = '2.0',HEADER_ROW=TRUE
)
WITH --withcolumn function
(   prodid int,
	loaddt date,
    amt int )
as txns;

--Json data Analytics
--Basic/Complex JSON Data Read using CSV trick, since direct JSON function doesn't work in Synapse - This query uses the "CSV trick" to read the entire JSON file as a single column.
--Complex way to access the nested json elements, not advisable to use!!!
SELECT *
,JSON_VALUE(jsondata, '$[0].shipment_id') AS shipment_id, --from_json() in spark
JSON_VALUE(jsondata, '$[0].order_id') AS order_id
FROM OPENROWSET(
    BULK 'https://we47toolstorage.dfs.core.windows.net/adfcontainer/sourcedir/logistics_shipment_detail_3000_1.json',
    FORMAT = 'CSV',  -- CSV trick to read raw JSON
    FIELDTERMINATOR = '0x0b',--dummy delimiter
    FIELDQUOTE = '0x0b',
    ROWTERMINATOR = '0x0b'
) 
WITH (
    jsondata NVARCHAR(MAX)
)
AS rows;


--Accessing Complex json
--CROSS APPLY OPENJSON(jsondata): This tells Synapse to iterate through each item in the JSON array (it will run for loop behind the scene)
--Internal WITH clause: This maps the specific JSON key ($.shipment_id) to a SQL column and assigns it the correct data type (INT).

SELECT 
    ship_id,
    order_id,
    source_city,
    destination_city,
    shipment_status,
    cargo_type,
    vehicle_type,
    payment_mode,
    shipment_weight_kg,
    shipment_cost,
    shipment_date
FROM OPENROWSET(
    BULK 'https://we47toolstorage.dfs.core.windows.net/adfcontainer/sourcedir/logistics_shipment_detail_3000_1.json',
    FORMAT = 'CSV',
    FIELDTERMINATOR = '0x0b',
    FIELDQUOTE = '0x0b',
    ROWTERMINATOR = '0x0b'
) 
WITH (
    jsondata NVARCHAR(MAX)
) AS src
CROSS APPLY OPENJSON(jsondata) --Explode Function - Iterating on the list of json objects, hence we don't need use index to read the respective rows
--$ = { "shipment_id": 5008467, "order_id": "ORD100000", "source_city": "Chennai", "destination_city": "Pune", "shipment_status": "CREATED", "cargo_type": "Electronics", "vehicle_type": "Bike", "payment_mode": "COD", "shipment_weight_kg": 476.12, "shipment_cost": 30695.8, "shipment_date": "24-04-23" }
WITH (
    ship_id INT '$.shipment_id', --parsing the current row json object
    order_id VARCHAR(100) '$.order_id',
    source_city VARCHAR(100) '$.source_city',
    destination_city VARCHAR(100) '$.destination_city',
    shipment_status VARCHAR(50) '$.shipment_status',
    cargo_type VARCHAR(100) '$.cargo_type',
    vehicle_type VARCHAR(50) '$.vehicle_type',
    payment_mode VARCHAR(50) '$.payment_mode',
    shipment_weight_kg FLOAT '$.shipment_weight_kg',
    shipment_cost FLOAT '$.shipment_cost',
    shipment_date VARCHAR(20) '$.shipment_date'
) AS rows;



-- How to use Synapse SQL Serverless Warehouse Openrowset to analyze data from ADLS Delta directly without creating table.
SELECT
    TOP 100 *
FROM
    OPENROWSET(
        BULK 'https://we47toolstorage.dfs.core.windows.net/adfcontainer/salesreportdelta/',
        FORMAT = 'DELTA'
    ) AS [result]


-- How to use Synapse SQL Serverless Warehouse Openrowset to analyze data from ADLS Parquet directly without creating table.
SELECT
    TOP 100 *
FROM
    OPENROWSET(
        BULK 'abfss://container1@we47toolstorage.dfs.core.windows.net/we47adfdata/inceptez_insurance.claim_transactions.parquet',
        FORMAT = 'PARQUET'
    ) AS [result]

--Concepts we are learning in this usecase 
-- 1. Learning about usage of External Data Source in the OPENROWSET function
-- 2. Learning about usage of External Data Source, External File Format & External Table Concept

use wd36db;

--to list the database names
select name from sys.databases;


--How the table is accessed hierarchy in the Azure Sysnapse: dbname.schemaname.tablename
select top 10 * from wd36schema.dbo.claims_parquet;

-- 1. Learning about usage of External Data Source in the OPENROWSET function
---------------------------------------------------------
-- CREATE EXTERNAL DATA SOURCE
---------------------------------------------------------

USE wd36db;

--DROP EXTERNAL DATA SOURCE raw_container;
--Ext Data source for BLOB storage (Not preferred)
CREATE EXTERNAL DATA SOURCE datasource_blob
WITH
(
    LOCATION = 'https://we47toolstorage.dfs.core.windows.net/container1/'
);

--Ext Data source for ADLS storage (preferred for performance)
CREATE EXTERNAL DATA SOURCE datasource_adls
WITH
(
    LOCATION = 'abfss://container1@we47toolstorage.dfs.core.windows.net/'
);


---------------------------------------------------------
-- READ CSV USING OPENROWSET function using EXTERNAL DATA SOURCE
---------------------------------------------------------
--Accessing BLOB data
SELECT *
FROM OPENROWSET(
    BULK 'synapsedata/custs',--Only folder and file we can just change, no need of using the fixed storage acct or container info everytime
    DATA_SOURCE = 'datasource_blob',--I don't require to give the complete uri of the ADLS/BLOB everytime in the openrowset
    FORMAT = 'CSV',
    HEADER_ROW = FALSE,
    PARSER_VERSION = '2.0'
) AS data1;

--Accessing ADLS data
SELECT *
FROM OPENROWSET(
    BULK 'synapsedata/custs',
    DATA_SOURCE = 'datasource_adls',
    FORMAT = 'CSV',
    HEADER_ROW = FALSE,
    PARSER_VERSION = '2.0'
) AS data1;


--Using openrowset With Header (equivalent to what in spark? schema(StructType(StructField))))
SELECT *
FROM OPENROWSET(
    BULK 'synapsedata/custs',
    DATA_SOURCE = 'datasource_adls',
    FORMAT = 'CSV',
    PARSER_VERSION = '2.0'
) 
WITH ( --schema(structtype(structfield....))
    CustomerID INT,
    FirstName VARCHAR(100),
    LastName VARCHAR(50),
    Age INT,
    Occupation VARCHAR(100)
) AS data1;


--2. Learning about usage of EXTERNAL FILE FORMAT + External Data Source + EXTERNAL TABLES

---------------------------------------------------------
-- CREATE EXTERNAL DATA SOURCE  to define the storage acct and container once for all
---------------------------------------------------------

CREATE EXTERNAL DATA SOURCE datasource_adls1
WITH
(
    LOCATION = 'abfss://container1@we47toolstorage.dfs.core.windows.net/'
);

---------------------------------------------------------
-- CREATE EXTERNAL FILE FORMAT  to define the file format, delimiter, header etc., once for all
---------------------------------------------------------
--This Command defines how Azure Synapse should interpret the structure of a flat file

--DROP EXTERNAL FILE FORMAT synapse_csv_format;
CREATE EXTERNAL FILE FORMAT csv_comma_noheader_format
WITH
(
    FORMAT_TYPE = DELIMITEDTEXT,
    FORMAT_OPTIONS
    (
        FIELD_TERMINATOR = ',',--delimiter/sep in spark
        FIRST_ROW = 1 --Read the data from line 1 onwards (since we don't have header)
    )
);


---------------------------------------------------------
-- CREATE EXTERNAL TABLE once for all to allow our analytics users or Power BI dashboard, to access the underlying ADLS datalake data..
---------------------------------------------------------

CREATE EXTERNAL TABLE ext_cust_table
(
    CustomerID INT,
    FirstName VARCHAR(50),
    LastName VARCHAR(50),
    Age INT,
    Occupation VARCHAR(100)
)
WITH
(
    LOCATION = 'synapsedata/custs',
    DATA_SOURCE = datasource_adls1,
    FILE_FORMAT = csv_comma_noheader_format
);

select * from ext_cust_table;--Behind the scene, structure come from metadata(sqlserver), data come from ADLS, compute come from Synapse Serverless


---------------------------------------------------------
-- CREATE EXTERNAL TABLE (with first row header): 
---------------------------------------------------------

CREATE EXTERNAL FILE FORMAT synapse_csv_format_header
WITH
(
    FORMAT_TYPE = DELIMITEDTEXT,
    FORMAT_OPTIONS
    (
        FIELD_TERMINATOR = ',',
        FIRST_ROW = 2 --Read the data from 2nd row onwards
    )
);

--drop external table ext_cust_tbl_header; --Very very important interview question - Drop will only drop the table structure but not the data
CREATE EXTERNAL TABLE ext_cust_tbl_header
(
    custid INT,
    fname VARCHAR(50),
    lname VARCHAR(50),
    age INT,
    prof VARCHAR(100)--inside the file, header has profession, i am overriding with prof
)
WITH
(
    LOCATION = 'synapsedata/custs_header',
    DATA_SOURCE = datasource_adls1,
    FILE_FORMAT = synapse_csv_format_header);

---------------------------------------------------------
-- QUERY EXTERNAL TABLE
---------------------------------------------------------

select * from ext_cust_tbl_header;

--Very very important interview question? CTE means what or have you used CTE queries?
--CTE - Common Table Expression 


--Inline View/From Clause Subquery (extracting data from ext_cust_tbl_header twice to reuse as t1 and t3)
--Not performant or good practice of writing query in general.
--select * from (some query here) as temptbl;

select t1.*,t2.* from 
(select * from ext_cust_tbl_header) t1
inner join (select * from ext_cust_tbl_header) t2
on t1.custid=t2.custid
union 
select t3.* from (select * from ext_cust_tbl_header) t3;

--Common table expression (extracting data from ext_cust_tbl_header only once and reuse as t1 multiple times in the given query)
--CTE
-- rather than writing like this (inline view), select * from (some query here) as temptbl;
-- write the query like this (CTE), with temptbl as (some query here) select * from temptl1;

with t1 as (select * from ext_cust_tbl_header),
t2 as (select * from ext_cust_tbl_header)
select t1.*,t2.* from t1 inner join t2 on t1.custid=t2.custid
union 
select t1.*,1 custid,'x' fname,'y' lname,1 age,'x' prof from t1
UNION
select t2.*,1 custid,'x' fname,'y' lname,1 age,'x' prof from t2;

-- Enabling JSON data for Analytics users in an optimal format ready for Analytics using CETAS
--EXTRACT Raw array of JSON Files (ADLS) using OPENROWSET -> Curation using  CROSS APPLY OPENJSON Parsing -> 
-- Using CETAS we are going to LOAD data in a External Table (Parquet Optimized Files) -> Consumption Analytics / Power BI

use wd36db;

CREATE EXTERNAL DATA SOURCE logistics_ds
WITH (
    LOCATION = 'https://we47toolstorage.dfs.core.windows.net/adfcontainer'
);

CREATE EXTERNAL FILE FORMAT parquet_format
WITH (
    FORMAT_TYPE = PARQUET );

--CETAS is used to Extract (json), Transform (json to structured) & Load the data into ADLS (parquet)
CREATE EXTERNAL TABLE ext_logistics_shipments_parquet
WITH (
    LOCATION = 'curated2/logistics_shipments/',
    DATA_SOURCE = logistics_ds,
    FILE_FORMAT = parquet_format
)
AS

SELECT 
    shipment_id,
    order_id,
    source_city,
    SUBSTRING(source_city,1,2) city_cd,
    destination_city,
    shipment_status,
    cargo_type,
    vehicle_type,
    payment_mode,
    shipment_weight_kg,
    shipment_cost,
    shipment_date

FROM OPENROWSET(
    BULK 'sourcedir/logistics_shipment_detail_3000_1.json',
    DATA_SOURCE = 'logistics_ds',
    FORMAT = 'CSV',
    FIELDTERMINATOR = '0x0b',
    FIELDQUOTE = '0x0b',
    ROWTERMINATOR = '0x0b'
) 
WITH (
    jsondata NVARCHAR(MAX)
) AS src

CROSS APPLY OPENJSON(jsondata)
WITH (
    shipment_id INT '$.shipment_id',
    order_id VARCHAR(100) '$.order_id',
    source_city VARCHAR(100) '$.source_city',
    destination_city VARCHAR(100) '$.destination_city',
    shipment_status VARCHAR(50) '$.shipment_status',
    cargo_type VARCHAR(100) '$.cargo_type',
    vehicle_type VARCHAR(50) '$.vehicle_type',
    payment_mode VARCHAR(50) '$.payment_mode',
    shipment_weight_kg FLOAT '$.shipment_weight_kg',
    shipment_cost FLOAT '$.shipment_cost',
    shipment_date VARCHAR(20) '$.shipment_date'
) AS rows;

select * from ext_logistics_shipments_parquet;--client3 can access this data directly from the table in an optimized fashion (parquet format)

--Expose Only Required Columns & rows to business creating views
CREATE OR ALTER VIEW vw_logistics_shipments_client1 
--View is a stored query to reduce complexity of writing queries again and again or for applying column/row level restrictions for securing my data etc.,
AS
SELECT
    shipment_id,
    order_id,
    source_city,
    destination_city,
    shipment_status
FROM ext_logistics_shipments_parquet;

select * from vw_logistics_shipments_client1;

CREATE OR ALTER VIEW vw_delivered_shipments_client2
AS
SELECT *
FROM ext_logistics_shipments_parquet
WHERE shipment_status = 'Delivered';

select * from vw_delivered_shipments_client2;


--0. Create a master (encrypted password) key to create scoped credential
CREATE MASTER KEY ENCRYPTION BY PASSWORD = 'Incepteztechnologies@123';

-- 1. Create the Database Scoped Credential using Managed Identity
-- Note: 'Managed Identity' is a reserved keyword here; do not change it.
CREATE DATABASE SCOPED CREDENTIAL creds_mi
WITH IDENTITY = 'Managed Identity';
GO

-- 2. Create the External Data Source pointing to your ADLS
-- Use the '.dfs.' endpoint for ADLS Gen2 performance
CREATE EXTERNAL DATA SOURCE bankdata_source_mi
WITH
(
    LOCATION = 'https://izstorageacct1.blob.core.windows.net/thirdpartycontainer/sourcedata/',
    CREDENTIAL = creds_mi
);
GO

-- 3. Test the connection by querying a file
SELECT *
FROM OPENROWSET(
    BULK 'custs',
    DATA_SOURCE = 'bankdata_source_mi',
    FORMAT = 'CSV',
    HEADER_ROW = FALSE,
    PARSER_VERSION = '2.0'
) AS data1;

source: ## Read Raw data from Data Lake(ADLS) to Datawarehouse(Lake Database)
spark.sql("DROP DATABASE IF EXISTS hranalytics_db CASCADE")

#Step 1: Create Database

spark.sql("CREATE DATABASE IF NOT EXISTS hranalytics_db")

spark.sql("""CREATE TABLE hranalytics_db.dim_employee (
   emp_id STRING,
   name STRING,
   dept STRING,
   role STRING,
   location STRING,
   hire_date DATE,
   salary DOUBLE,
   status STRING,
   start_date DATE,
   end_date DATE,
   is_current BOOLEAN
)""")

#Step 2: Define Path
base_path = "abfss://container1@we47storage1.dfs.core.windows.net/hr-rawdata/"

#Step 3: Read CSV Files
employee_df = spark.read.format("csv") \
    .option("header", True) \
    .option("inferSchema", True) \
    .load(base_path + "employee.csv")

performance_df = spark.read.format("csv") \
    .option("header", True) \
    .option("inferSchema", True) \
    .load(base_path + "performance.csv")

survey_df = spark.read.format("csv") \
    .option("header", True) \
    .option("inferSchema", True) \
    .load(base_path + "survey.csv")

exit_df = spark.read.format("csv") \
    .option("header", True) \
    .option("inferSchema", True) \
    .load(base_path + "exit.csv")

#Step 4: Add Bronze Metadata Columns
from pyspark.sql.functions import current_timestamp, lit

def add_bronze_columns(df, source_name):
    return df.withColumn("ingestion_time", current_timestamp()) \
             .withColumn("source_file", lit(source_name))

employee_df = add_bronze_columns(employee_df, "employee.csv")
performance_df = add_bronze_columns(performance_df, "performance.csv")
survey_df = add_bronze_columns(survey_df, "survey.csv")
exit_df = add_bronze_columns(exit_df, "exit.csv")

#Step 5: Write to Bronze Tables (Delta)
employee_df.write.format("delta") \
    .mode("overwrite") \
    .saveAsTable("hranalytics_db.bronze_employee")

performance_df.write.format("delta") \
    .mode("overwrite") \
    .saveAsTable("hranalytics_db.bronze_performance")

survey_df.write.format("delta") \
    .mode("overwrite") \
    .saveAsTable("hranalytics_db.bronze_survey")

exit_df.write.format("delta") \
    .mode("overwrite") \
    .saveAsTable("hranalytics_db.bronze_exit")

#Step 6: Validate
spark.sql("SELECT COUNT(*) FROM hranalytics_db.bronze_employee").show()
spark.sql("SELECT COUNT(*) FROM hranalytics_db.bronze_performance").show()
spark.sql("SELECT COUNT(*) FROM hranalytics_db.bronze_survey").show()
spark.sql("SELECT COUNT(*) FROM hranalytics_db.bronze_exit").show()

df = spark.read.format("csv").option("inferschema",True).load("abfss://container1@we47storage1.dfs.core.windows.net/sourcedir/txns") \
.toDF("txnid","txndate","custid","amount","category","product","city","state","paytype")
df.printSchema()
df.show()
%%pyspark
spark.sql("update  hranalytics_db.silver_employee set location = 'Chennai', salary = 500000 where emp_id = 'E053'")

df = spark.read.format("csv").option("inferschema",True).load("abfss://container1@we47storage1.dfs.core.windows.net/sourcedir/txns") \
.toDF("txnid","txndate","custid","amount","category","product","city","state","paytype")
df.printSchema()
df.show()
## 2_data_process

### Objective of Silver Layer
- Clean nulls & invalid data
- Standardize column names & formats
- Deduplicate records
- Join datasets for analytics-ready structure

#Step 1: Read Bronze Tables
employee_df = spark.read.table("hranalytics_db.bronze_employee")
performance_df = spark.read.table("hranalytics_db.bronze_performance")
survey_df = spark.read.table("hranalytics_db.bronze_survey")
exit_df = spark.read.table("hranalytics_db.bronze_exit")

#Step 2: Standardize Column Names
from pyspark.sql.functions import col

def standardize_columns(df):
    for c in df.columns:
        df = df.withColumnRenamed(c, c.strip().lower().replace(" ", "_"))        
    
    #drop columns
    df = df.drop("ingestion_time", "source_file")
    return df

employee_df = standardize_columns(employee_df)
performance_df = standardize_columns(performance_df)
survey_df = standardize_columns(survey_df)
exit_df = standardize_columns(exit_df)

#Step 3: Data Cleaning
# Employee Cleaning 
"""
1. Drop Duplicates
2. Datatype Conversion
3. Replace null values with default value
4. Remove rows where empid is null
"""
from pyspark.sql.functions import when

employee_clean = employee_df.dropDuplicates(["emp_id"]) \
    .withColumn("salary", col("salary").cast("double")) \
    .fillna({
        "dept": "Unknown",
        "role": "Unknown"
    }) \
    .filter(col("emp_id").isNotNull())

#Performance Cleaning
performance_clean = performance_df.dropDuplicates() \
    .withColumn("rating", col("rating").cast("int")) \
    .filter(col("emp_id").isNotNull())

#Survey Cleaning (eNPS prep)
survey_clean = survey_df.dropDuplicates() \
    .withColumn("survey_score", col("survey_score").cast("int")) \
    .withColumn("enps_category",
        when(col("survey_score") >= 9, "Promoter")
        .when(col("survey_score") >= 5, "Passive")
        .otherwise("Detractor")
    )

#Exit Data Cleaning
exit_clean = exit_df.dropDuplicates() \
    .filter(col("emp_id").isNotNull()) \
    .withColumnRenamed("reason", "exit_reason")

employee_clean.write.format("delta") \
    .mode("overwrite") \
    .option("overwriteSchema", "true") \
    .saveAsTable("hranalytics_db.silver_employee")

performance_clean.write.format("delta") \
    .mode("overwrite") \
    .option("overwriteSchema", "true") \
    .saveAsTable("hranalytics_db.silver_performance")

survey_clean.write.format("delta") \
    .mode("overwrite") \
    .option("overwriteSchema", "true") \
    .saveAsTable("hranalytics_db.silver_survey")

exit_clean.write.format("delta") \
    .mode("overwrite") \
    .option("overwriteSchema", "true") \
    .saveAsTable("hranalytics_db.silver_exit")
## 3_dim_emp_scd2

#Step 1: Read Tables
from pyspark.sql.functions import current_date, lit, col

df = spark.table("hranalytics_db.silver_employee").alias("src")
dim_df = spark.table("hranalytics_db.dim_employee").alias("tgt")

#Step 2: Detect Changed Records
changed_df = df.join(dim_df, col("src.emp_id") == col("tgt.emp_id")) \
   .filter(
       (col("tgt.is_current") == True) & (
           (col("src.salary") != col("tgt.salary")) |
           (col("src.role") != col("tgt.role")) |
           (col("src.dept") != col("tgt.dept")) |
           (col("src.location") != col("tgt.location"))
       )
   ).select("src.*")

#Step 3: Detect New Employees (Insert)
new_df = df.join(dim_df, "emp_id", "left_anti")

#Step 4: Expire Old Records
expired_df = dim_df.alias("tgt") \
   .join(changed_df.alias("src"), "emp_id") \
   .filter(col("tgt.is_current") == True) \
   .select("tgt.*") \
   .withColumn("end_date", current_date()) \
   .withColumn("is_current", lit(False))
expired_df.printSchema()

#Step 5: Prepare New Records (for both changed + new)
insert_df = changed_df.union(new_df) \
   .withColumn("start_date", current_date()) \
   .withColumn("end_date", lit(None)) \
   .withColumn("is_current", lit(True))
insert_df.printSchema()

#Final Merge Logic
# Keep unchanged records
unchanged_df = dim_df.alias("tgt") \
   .join(changed_df.alias("src"), "emp_id", "left_anti")
unchanged_df.printSchema()

# Final dataset
final_df = unchanged_df.union(expired_df).union(insert_df)

# Write to temp
final_df.write.mode("overwrite").saveAsTable("hranalytics_db.employee_tmp")

#Write back
df_tmp = spark.table("hranalytics_db.employee_tmp")
df_tmp.write.mode("overwrite").saveAsTable("hranalytics_db.dim_employee")

content.query:
select * from dim_employee;

select * from fact_employee_metrics;

source: ## 4_build_fact_table

#Step 1: Read Silver Tables

from pyspark.sql.functions import col, when, lit

# Read Silver tables
emp_df = spark.table("hranalytics_db.silver_employee")
perf_df = spark.table("hranalytics_db.silver_performance")
survey_df = spark.table("hranalytics_db.silver_survey")
exit_df = spark.table("hranalytics_db.silver_exit")

#Step 2: Read Current Dimension (SCD2)
#Always use only current records
dim_df = spark.table("hranalytics_db.dim_employee") \
              .filter(col("is_current") == True)

#Step 3: Join All Sources
fact_df = dim_df.alias("d") \
    .join(perf_df.alias("p"), col("d.emp_id") == col("p.emp_id"), "left") \
    .join(survey_df.alias("s"), col("d.emp_id") == col("s.emp_id"), "left") \
    .join(exit_df.alias("e"), col("d.emp_id") == col("e.emp_id"), "left")


#Step 4: Derive Metrics Columns

fact_df = fact_df.select(
    col("d.emp_id").alias("emp_sk"),
    col("p.rating"),
    col("s.survey_score"),
    col("p.review_date"),
    col("s.survey_date"),
    col("e.exit_date"),
    
    # Attrition Flag
    when(col("e.emp_id").isNotNull(), lit(1)).otherwise(lit(0)).alias("attrition_flag"),
    
    # Engagement Bucket
    when(col("s.survey_score") >= 8, "High")
    .when(col("s.survey_score") >= 5, "Medium")
    .otherwise("Low").alias("engagement_level"),
    
    # Risk Flag (Early Churn Detection)
    when((col("p.rating") <= 2) & (col("s.survey_score") <= 5), 1)
    .otherwise(0).alias("churn_risk_flag")
)

#Step 5: Handle Duplicates (Important)

#If multiple records per employee (e.g., multiple reviews)

from pyspark.sql.window import Window
from pyspark.sql.functions import row_number

window_spec = Window.partitionBy("emp_sk").orderBy(col("review_date").desc())

fact_df = fact_df.withColumn("rn", row_number().over(window_spec)) \
                 .filter(col("rn") == 1) \
                 .drop("rn")

#Step 6: Write to Gold Layer
fact_df.write.mode("overwrite").saveAsTable("hranalytics_db.fact_employee_metrics")
## 5_kpi_analytics

#-- Attrition Rate
spark.sql("""
SELECT 
    ROUND(COUNT(CASE WHEN attrition_flag = 1 THEN 1 END) * 100.0 / COUNT(*),2) AS attrition_rate
FROM hranalytics_db.fact_employee_metrics
""").show()

# -- Resignation Drivers
spark.sql("""
SELECT exit_reason, COUNT(*) AS cnt
FROM hranalytics_db.silver_exit
GROUP BY exit_reason
ORDER BY cnt DESC
""").show()

#-- Engagement Score
spark.sql(
    """
SELECT AVG(survey_score) AS avg_engagement
FROM hranalytics_db.fact_employee_metrics
"""
).show()

#-- eNPS Score
spark.sql(
    """
    SELECT 
    ROUND((COUNT(CASE WHEN survey_score >= 9 THEN 1 END) -
     COUNT(CASE WHEN survey_score <= 6 THEN 1 END)) * 100.0 / COUNT(*),2) AS eNPS
FROM hranalytics_db.fact_employee_metrics
"""
).show()

#-- Early Churn Detection
spark.sql("""

SELECT emp_sk
FROM hranalytics_db.fact_employee_metrics
WHERE rating < 2 AND survey_score < 5 and exit_date is not null

"""
).show()
